items.q: Which linguistic term matches this definition: Rules governing sentence structure?
Which linguistic term matches this definition: Using a part to represent the whole or vice versa?
Which linguistic term matches this definition: Conventional spelling system of a language?
Which linguistic term matches this definition: Change in formality based on audience or purpose?
items.explanation: The matching term is register shift.

items.q: A phoneme is a full word with dictionary meaning.
items.explanation: A phoneme is a sound unit.

items.a: Alternating between language varieties based on context
options: Alternating between language varieties based on context
A morpheme that can stand alone as a word
Using a part to represent the whole or vice versa
items.explanation: code-switching means Alternating between language varieties based on context.
Use the linguistic definition of semantics.

items.q: Code-switching can occur within the same sentence.

items.a: Replacing something with a related term
items.explanation: Use the linguistic definition of metonymy.

items.a: Words whose meaning depends on context like here or now
items.explanation: Use the linguistic definition of deixis.
